concerns.text: Figure 1 (citevqa_example.pdf) and Figure 2 (citevqa_pipeline.pdf) lack explicit axis labels or unit definitions in their captions. The caption for Fig 1(b) mentions 'Dataset statistics' but does not specify the units for 'page counts' or 'document scale' (e.g., is it mean, median, or max?). The caption for Fig 2 describes the workflow but does not define the units for 'semantic alignment' or 'evidence chain' metrics if they are plotted. Ensure all axes in the source PDFs have clear labels and units, or update captions to explicitly state them.

Figure 3 (question_statistics.pdf) caption states 'Evidence Locality (in relative percent)' but does not specify the scale (0-100% or 0-1.0) or the specific metric being plotted (e.g., % of text vs. % of tables). The 'Cross-page Span' axis lacks a unit (e.g., number of pages). Update the figure source to include clear axis labels with units, or refine the caption to define these metrics precisely.

Case Study Figures (Simple_Case.pdf, Case_Study1.pdf, Case_Study2.pdf) are referenced in the text (lines 345, 360, 375) but the captions do not explicitly define the coordinate system or scale of the 'image crops' used to replace cited coordinates. The caption for Fig Simple_Case states 'cited coordinates are replaced with image crops' but does not specify the resolution or scale of these crops, which is critical for evaluating 'element-level' bounding box fidelity. Add a note in the caption or figure about the resolution/scale of the crops.

Figure 4 (citevqa_ability_radar.pdf) and Figure 5 (citevqa_domain_radar.pdf) are radar charts. The captions do not specify the scale of the axes (e.g., 0-100% SAA score) or the specific models being compared in each chart. Ensure the radar chart axes are clearly labeled with units (e.g., 'SAA Score (%)') and that the legend or caption explicitly lists the models included in each chart to avoid ambiguity.

All figures (Fig 1-5) are referenced in the text but do not have alt text defined in the LaTeX source. While not strictly required for print, it is a best practice for accessibility. Add lttext{...} or similar commands if the document class supports it, or ensure the captions are sufficiently descriptive to serve as alt text for screen readers.
